IOT.guidance_note: FY26 revenue guidance of $1.547-$1.555B implies 24-25% growth, down from prior 31% growth trajectory due to macro uncertainty and tariff-related customer delays
bullets: Q1 ARR reached $1.54 billion, growing 31% year-over-year constant currency, with revenue of $367 million up 31% YoY
Added 154 customers with $100K+ ARR in Q1 (35% YoY growth), reaching 2,638 total large customers representing 58% of ARR mix
Record gross margin of 79% and operating margin of 14% (up from 2% in Q1 FY25), demonstrating strong profitability expansion
International contributed 18% of net new ACV, tied for highest ever, with Europe showing particular strength
95% of $100K+ ARR customers subscribe to 2+ products, 66% subscribe to 3+ products, with dollar-based net retention of ~115%
Experienced tens of millions in deal slippage from Q1 to Q2 following Liberation Day in April as customers prioritized tariff-impacted asset purchases
risks: Elongated sales cycles due to macro uncertainty and customers prioritizing spending on tariff-impacted goods (vehicles, equipment, assets) following Liberation Day in April
Near-term timing risk on deal closures despite strong pipeline generation and consistent win rates
Customer feedback indicating supply chain resilience challenges and concerns about tariff impacts on asset procurement costs and lead times

HIVE.guidance_note: Targeting 25 exahash by November 2025 (from current 11.5 exahash) and scaling HPC business to $100 million ARR in 2026 from current $20 million run rate.
bullets: Full-year revenue of $115.3 million ($105 million Bitcoin mining, $10 million HPC AI); adjusted EBITDA of $56.2 million with 22% ROIC, leading the sector
Successfully scaled hash rate from 4.7 exahash at March 2024 to 11.5 exahash in June 2025, mining 5.5 Bitcoin daily ($600k/day revenue at $200 million ARR)
Fully funded expansion to 25 exahash by fall 2025 targeting 3% of global Bitcoin network; projected $400+ million ARR at $100k Bitcoin with $250+ million gross mining margin
HPC business hit $20 million ARR in May 2025 (up 300% YoY); acquired 7.2 MW Toronto data center to 3.5x HPC footprint targeting $100 million ARR in 2026
Balance sheet holds $181.1 million in digital assets (610 Bitcoin worth $65+ million) plus $23.4 million cash; pledged to buy back 1,300 Bitcoin at $87k to exceed 2,000 Bitcoin by year-end
Gross mining margin declined to 22% from 33% YoY due to April 2024 halving impact, but company targets sub-1-year ASIC ROI with $42k cost to produce Bitcoin at 25 exahash
risks: Net loss of $0.02 per share for fiscal 2025 compared to $0.29 profit prior year, driven by Bitcoin halving in April 2024 reducing mining rewards by 50%
Rising Bitcoin network difficulty and hash rates continuing to pressure gross mining margins despite scaling operations
HPC data center conversion timeline uncertainty with 9-12 month retrofit required for liquid cooling installation at Toronto facility

commentary.overnight: Bitcoin collapsed below $61,000 overnight (now ~$60,950), down 3.6% in 24h and 17% over 7 days, its weakest level since October 2024. Friday's blowout 172,000 payroll print doubled expectations, pushing 10-year yields to 4.54% and rate-hike odds to 57%, triggering a brutal risk-off rout. Major indices bled hard: S&P 500 closed at 7,384 (-2.64%), Nasdaq at 25,709 (-4.18%), with the VIX spiking 40%.
commentary.positions: Your crypto miners and quantum plays got crushed. BTBT (-11.35%), GLXY (-11.51%), and HIVE (-13.73%) tracked Bitcoin's nosedive, though HIVE and MARA remain profitable at +42.3% and +30.1% G/L. IREN and RIOT both sit within 6-7% of stop-losses despite strong Ichimoku signals. Quantum stocks (IONQ -13.52%, QBTS -13.71%, RGTI -14.40%) tumbled on profit-taking, higher yields, and Quantinuum's weak IPO debut at $62 after opening at $68. SMCI dropped 11.22% on the Friday jobs selloff and Broadcom's disappointing AI chip guidance, extending tech sector pain. ORCL reports June 10.
commentary.earnings: IOT beat Q1 EPS by 30.6% ($0.17 vs $0.1302 est) on June 4 but lowered guidance, stock down only 1.16% suggesting resilience. HIVE missed Q4 EPS badly (-$0.28 vs -$0.21 est) but maintained bullish tone on hash rate expansion to 25 exahash. Oracle reports Tuesday June 10 with EPS est of $1.96; analysts citing a $553B backlog.
commentary.macro: CPI prints Wednesday June 10 (est: 0.5% MoM, 4.2% YoY, Core 0.3%/2.9%) will be critical with markets already pricing 57% odds of rate hikes this year. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh takes podium at June 17 FOMC for first time in 8 years, potentially adding volatility as investors gauge hawkish tone.
